title: P2 docs + attention toast + merge-lanes test: mixed runners
prompt: Complete remaining P2 backlog: layered review docs (#14), phase fanout templates (#15), attention toast (#16), merge-lanes integration test (#22). Uses mixed runners to dogfood pi-direct with different models. All work must pass WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'.

jobs.name: impl-attention-toast
jobs.prompt:
Implement attention toast notifications for the workflow TUI (#16). Do NOT launch ccc or other runners — work directly.

WHAT TO BUILD:
The TUI overview tab already shows attention items (health warnings). Now add a TOAST NOTIFICATION system:
1. When NEW attention items appear (items not seen in the previous TUI refresh), show a compact toast/notification at the top of the TUI for about 7.5 seconds, then auto-dismiss.
2. Track 'seen' state: on each TUI refresh, compare current attention items to the previous refresh. New items get a toast. Items that were already present do NOT re-trigger.
3. The toast should be a Rich Panel (maybe with a yellow/dim border) showing the new attention item message. Position it at the top of the layout, above the header.
4. In the overview sidebar (make_attention_table), mark NEW/unread items with a visual indicator (e.g., a bullet or bold styling) until they are 'seen' (i.e., have been present for at least one refresh cycle).
5. The toast state MUST live in scripts/workflow_tui_update.py (do NOT create a new module — a new file would be outside write_scope and fail merge-back). Use a simple dict tracking item_key → first_seen_timestamp. Items older than 15 seconds are 'seen' and no longer toast-eligible. Source the current time from workflow_tui_render.snapshot_reference_time() (which reads the WORKFLOW_TUI_SNAPSHOT_NOW env var) so tests can drive the clock; fall back to datetime.now() only when that env var is unset.
6. Keep it under 200 LOC for the toast system itself.

Add a test in tests/test_workflow.py: create a fixture with attention items, render the TUI twice — first render shows the toast for new items, second render (after items age past 15s) does not. The test MUST be deterministic: drive the clock via WORKFLOW_TUI_SNAPSHOT_NOW (the env var backing snapshot_reference_time()) — set it to T0 for the first render, then advance it to T0+16s for the second render. Do NOT sleep in real time. Use snapshot_env() conventions.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' and fix until all pass.

jobs.name: impl-merge-lanes-test
jobs.prompt:
Add a comprehensive integration test for merge-lanes auto-resolve (#22). Do NOT launch ccc or other runners — work directly.

WHAT TO BUILD:
There is already a test `test_merge_lanes_auto_resolve_dispatches_resolver_agent` but it uses WORKFLOW_NO_RESOLVE=1 and only checks that the resolver is dispatched, not that it actually resolves. Add a NEW test that exercises the full auto-resolve path WITHOUT the no-resolve flag:
1. Create a workflow run with two worktree lanes that both edit the SAME file (different parts).
2. Call merge-lanes (without WORKFLOW_NO_RESOLVE=1).
3. Mock the resolver agent (don't use a real ccc process — use a fake that resolves by accepting the conflict markers).
4. Verify the merge succeeds and the lane is marked merged.
5. Also add a test for the edge case where the resolver FAILS (mock returns non-zero) — verify the merge is aborted and the lane stays conflicted.
6. Use mocks/fakes — no real model calls.

Add tests in a NEW file tests/test_workflow_merge_resolve.py (do NOT edit tests/test_workflow.py — a parallel job owns it and merge-back would conflict). Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' and fix until all pass.

jobs.name: write-docs
jobs.prompt:
Write documentation for two remaining P2 backlog items. Do NOT launch ccc or other runners — work directly.

#14: ERGONOMIC LAYERED REVIEW WORKFLOWS
Create references/layered-review-workflows.md documenting:
- How to declare an implementation + ordered multi-model review stack in ONE workflow-plan JSON
- The preferred pattern: phase-level ccc_runner overrides (impl phase uses @mimo25p, review-1 uses @glm51, review-2 uses @mm3)
- Example workflow-plan JSON with 3 phases: impl, semantic-review, standards-review
- The 'layered model stack can still miss edge cases' warning from the backlog — keep lead-level review mandatory
- How to rotate review model order between runs for diversity

#15: REAL MULTI-AGENT PHASE DOGFOOD
Create references/multi-agent-phase-dogfood.md documenting:
- How to declare multiple implementation agents in the SAME phase with disjoint worktrees/scopes
- Example workflow-plan JSON with a fan-out phase (3 parallel impl agents) → synthesis phase → review phase
- Guidance on when to use phase fan-out vs sequential phases
- How worktree lanes prevent merge conflicts between parallel agents
- The dependency graph: all jobs in a phase become deps for the next phase

Both docs should reference the existing workflow-patterns.md for the fan-out/layered patterns. Keep docs concise (under 200 lines each). Include working JSON examples that use ONLY real schema fields (kind, title, cwd, runner, ccc_runner, model, max_agents, phases[].phase_id/name/depends_on/jobs, and per-job name/prompt/runner/ccc_runner/model/write_scope/worktree) — verify each example loads cleanly through workflow_plan.normalize_workflow before finishing.

Also add brief pointers to SKILL.md under 'Delegation Rules' or a new 'Advanced Patterns' section.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' to ensure nothing broke.

jobs.name: review-semantic
jobs.prompt: Semantic review. Verify: (1) attention toast triggers on new items and dismisses after ~7.5s, (2) merge-lanes integration test covers both success and failure paths, (3) docs are accurate with working JSON examples. Run WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'. Fix issues or file findings. Return strict JSON: status, findings, tests_passed. Do NOT launch ccc or other runners.

jobs.name: review-standards
jobs.prompt: Standards review. Check LOC limits, no duplication, meaningful names, test quality, snapshot freshness, docs quality. Run WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'. Fix issues. Return strict JSON: status, findings, tests_passed. Do NOT launch ccc or other runners.
